entities.name: CARBON SINK
entities.description: A reservoir that accumulates and stores carbon-containing chemical compounds to alleviate climate change effects

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns affecting agriculture

entities.name: SEMIARID TROPICAL DRYLANDS
entities.description: Regions in the tropics characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Approaches to managing soil nutrients to maintain productivity and sequester carbon

entities.name: CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide in the soil

entities.name: CHEMICAL FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Synthetic nutrient substances applied to soil to increase crop productivity

entities.name: SOIL TEST-BASED RECOMMENDATIONS
entities.description: Nutrient application rates determined by the specific nutrient levels found in soil tests

entities.name: BIOCHAR
entities.description: A charcoal-like substance produced by pyrolysis of organic matter used as a soil amendment

entities.name: COMPOST
entities.description: Decomposed organic matter used as a soil amendment and nutrient source

entities.name: INTEGRATED NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A combined application of chemical fertilizers and organic amendments like biochar or compost

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal grain crop used in the cropping sequence

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Cicer arietinum L.) used in the cropping sequence

entities.name: ORGANIC CARBON STOCK
entities.description: The amount of organic carbon stored in the top layer of the soil

entities.name: RESIDUAL EFFECT
entities.description: The continuing effect of a previous nutrient treatment on a subsequent crop

entities.name: SYSTEM EQUIVALENT YIELD
entities.description: A measure of total productivity across a cropping sequence

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The carbon component of soil organic matter

entities.name: LABILE CARBON CONCENTRATIONS
entities.description: The fraction of soil organic carbon that is easily decomposed by microorganisms

entities.name: MICROBIAL BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of microorganisms present in the soil

entities.name: NET RETURNS
entities.description: The financial gain after subtracting costs from total revenue

entities.name: BENEFITS-COST RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of total benefits to total costs of a nutrient management strategy

entities.name: LAND DEGRADATION
entities.description: The decline in soil quality and productivity in drylands

entities.name: NUTRIENT IMBALANCES
entities.description: The lack of proper proportions of essential nutrients in the soil

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food

relationships.description: reduces yield when used as sole treatment
